reported_shape.description: Text preamble followed by <tool_call><function=exec_command></function></tool_call> with no <parameter=cmd> tag

commands: .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_server.py -k 'streaming_responses_tool_call_arguments_survive_buffering or streaming_responses_reasoning_tool_call_keeps_arguments or streaming_responses_tool_call_uses_next_output_index_without_text or streaming_responses_required_empty_xml_tool_call_is_rejected or streaming_responses_preamble_empty_xml_tool_call_never_emits_empty_arguments'
proven: Current source drops parsed tool calls whose request schema has missing required arguments.
The exact preamble plus empty XML function shape emits visible preamble text but no function_call item and no response.function_call_arguments delta/done events.
The exact shape returns a tool_calls_required error when tool_choice is required instead of emitting executable arguments {}.
Current source keeps non-empty tool arguments across buffering and reasoning-channel tool calls.
Current source uses the next output_index for function_call items after the placeholder message item.
not_proven: Public tunnel raw SSE parity remains unclosed because the stale tunnel capture still duplicates output_index 0.
This source proof does not close deployed Qwen/Qwen3.6/Qwen3-coder issue reports until direct/gateway/tunnel are recaptured on the same deployed build.
This does not prove every parser family; it covers the current Qwen/Responses empty-args subcase and existing focused cross-parser guards.
parallel_agent_next: Do not add a fallback that invents cmd from the preamble.
